message:
Redesign mobile navigation: bottom tab bar + side drawer

Major overhaul of the mobile navigation experience:

Desktop changes (minor):
- Removed the audio toggle button from the desktop toolbar since
  audio controls are accessible from the mobile sidebar and the
  terminal; reduces visual clutter in the primary nav
- Condensed JSX for toolbar buttons (single-line className props)
- Added text-left to the ALIVE status indicator container
- Updated section comments for clarity

Mobile changes (major):
- Replaces the top-mounted hamburger bar with a fixed bottom tab
  bar positioned at the thumb-reach zone (bottom-6), showing icon
  + label for the first 5 nav items plus a "MORE" overflow button
- New getIcon() function maps nav labels to lucide-react icons
  (Home, Eye, Cpu, Skull, Network, Info, Zap) for the tab bar
- Active tab shows a green glow background via Framer Motion
  layoutId animation ("mobile-active-glow")
- The "More" button opens a right-side drawer (slides from right)
  instead of the previous bottom-sheet (slid up from bottom),
  providing more vertical space for the full nav list and actions
- Drawer includes toggle buttons for Anatomy Mode and Audio in a
  2-column grid, plus the terminal launcher and GitHub CTA
- Backdrop changed from bg-black/80 to bg-black/90 with stronger
  backdrop-blur-md for better contrast
- New icon imports: Home, Network, LayoutGrid, Skull, Info, Cpu,
  ChevronRight
- Removed the Escape-key-closes-menu useEffect (mobile keyboards
  rarely have Escape; the backdrop tap and X button suffice)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
